transcript: This guy just landed ahead of AI role by vibe coding and AI system that evaluated 700 jobs for him and automated his job hunt. The crazy part that he made it open source so that anyone can use it for free. Here's what happened. A guy named Santiago wanted a job. So instead of doing what we all do, he opened Claude code and said, "Build me a system that does my entire job search for me." And Claude built it. You give it a job link. Claude reads the whole post in, scores how good the fit is, rewrites your resume specifically for that role with the right keywords so the company's software doesn't reject it, and turns it into a ready to send PDF. By the way, this is crazy AI update of the day, and today is day 16. Now, with the tool he coded, he didn't use it for 10 jobs or 50, 740 plus. That's how many jobs this system evaluated. Over a 100 custom RS generated, each one built from scratch. It scans 45 plus companies on its own. Anthropic, OpenAI, Stripe finds openings, checks the fit, fills out application forms, even gives you word for word scripts for when they lowball your salary. One guy, no team, no recruiter, just him and Claude code. And if you go to the project page right now, Claude is not listed as a tool. It's listed as a co-creator right next to his name. So what happened when he actually used it? He didn't just get a job. He landed ahead of applied AI role and then he did something nobody expected. He made the entire system open-source, 100% free. That means anyone watching this right now can use his exact system to hunt for job. 7,000 people have already grabbed it in 2 days. If you know someone who's looking for a job right now, send them this reel. Comment job and I will send you the link to my WhatsApp community where I have shared the full setup step by step in a document and also shared the link to the repo. It's free.

hook_text_english: GPT 5.5 just launched. This is not a smarter chatbot.
full_text_english: GPT 5.5 just launched. 🤯 And this is not a smarter chatbot. It is a completely different class of AI.Until yesterday, every AI worked the same way. You ask. It replies. You ask again. It replies again.GPT 5.5 does not work like that.You give it a goal. It opens tools. Browses the web. Writes code. Creates documents. Checks its own work. And keeps going until the entire job is done.Smarter than GPT 5.4. Just as fast. Uses fewer tokens. All at the same time.Here is the thing ↓ I built an entire Mac app with it.One prompt. It wrote the code, debugged the errors, fixed them, and shipped it. I did not touch the keyboard. It worked autonomously for 8 hours straight.This is not an assistant you talk to. This is a coworker that finishes the job.And the timing is brutal.Everyone thought Claude had the lead. OpenAI was "falling behind." Sam Altman was being counted out.GPT 5.5 just closed the gap. In one drop.The real question is not which AI is smarter anymore. It is who lets AI execute first.Whoever ships first, wins.I share updates like these in my free AI community on WhatsApp.Join here ↓ https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ

Would love to see how positioning data feeds into content decisions. 👇
Not another AI tool — this is a system

Super exciting direction for AI agents. The shift from “chatbot that answers” to “coworker that executes end‑to‑end” is exactly what will change how we build products and run businesses.Curious to see how teams adapt their workflows and guardrails when an AI can autonomously write code, ship features, and iterate for hours. Execution-first AI really might become the new competitive edge.
The interesting part is not autonomy itself, it’s how much oversight you need per hour of execution. That ratio decides if this scales or not.
Codex
I think GPT-5.5 isn’t an upgrade. It’s a mindset shift — from prompting tools to delegating outcomes. The gap between early adopters and everyone else just got wider.

The shift from AI as an assistant to AI as a coworker is the part that changes everything. Most people are still using AI like a faster search engine. The ones treating it like an autonomous agent are getting completely different results. What have you noticed in terms of adoption speed?
Most users won’t notice hype-level changes, but workflows will get faster and sharper.Feels like AI is quietly moving from impressive to actually indispensable

Vaibhav Sisinty Interesting shift. The real unlock isn’t smarter responses, it’s autonomous execution. When AI starts completing workflows end-to-end, the leverage for founders changes completely. The winners won’t be who uses AI, but who builds systems around it fastest.

GPT 5.5 as a coworker rather than an assistant is a game-changer for productivity. For FP&A specifically, imagine AI autonomously pulling data from multiple ERPs, reconciling, building dashboards, and flagging anomalies — all while you focus on the story behind the numbers.
The autonomy angle is powerful, but I think the real differentiator will be how reliably these systems execute in real-world constraints. Execution + trust = adoption.

The real question isn't GPT-5.5 vs Claude. It's: which AI can you trust with a task that has real consequences — a financial model, a legal document, a customer-facing product? That answer depends on your use case, not on launch day hype.

The shift from conversational AI to autonomous execution is what changes the game. Most people are still treating AI as a tool they need to guide step by step. The ones who'll see real productivity gains are the ones who learn to frame complete outcomes instead of individual tasks. That's a different skill set entirely — it's less about knowing the right prompt and more about knowing the right objective.
The way GPT 5.5 handled building your entire Mac app, including debugging, really blurs the line between tool and teammate. I'm curious how this shift will affect developer roles moving forward.
This is a big change: going from answering to actually doing things changes how people use AI in a big way.In practice, the real test will be how reliable and easy to understand those outputs are.

hook_text_english: SpaceX just bought right to acquire Cursor for 60 billion dollars
full_text_english: SpaceX just bought the right to acquire Cursor for 60 billion dollars.Cursor had a problem every founder secretly fears. every company selling them the tools to run their business was also trying to kill them. OpenAI. Anthropic. Google. their landlords were also their rivals.then Elon walked in with a massive GPU cluster and an escape hatch.here's what actually happened↓Cursor is one of the fastest growing software companies ever. zero to roughly 2 billion dollars in annualized revenue in about 3 years.to keep growing, they needed to train their own AI. but training AI needs massive computing power. and the only companies that have that power are the same ones building Cursor's biggest competitors.OpenAI has Codex. Anthropic has Claude Code. Google has Gemini CLI.Cursor was paying its rivals rent while those rivals tried to replace them.then SpaceX made the offer nobody saw coming.Elon's company has been building one of the biggest AI compute clusters on earth. they just handed a massive chunk of it to Cursor.in return, SpaceX got two things.one. the right to buy Cursor for 60 billion dollars one year from now. roughly 15% above Cursor's current valuation.two. if SpaceX walks away and never buys Cursor. they still pay 10 billion dollars just for the partnership.read that again. 10 billion dollars just for walking away.that is more than Cursor was valued at a year ago.here's what nobody is saying.this was never a straight acquisition. it's a compute deal dressed up as one.SpaceX didn't need a code editor. Cursor didn't need money. they traded the one thing they each had in excess. compute for Cursor. distribution and product for SpaceX.and Elon just bought a one-year call option on the future of coding for 10 billion dollars.that's the deal.
body_summary_english: Cursor, a fast-growing software company, was acquired by SpaceX to escape vendor lock-in from AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic, enabling independent AI training capabilities.
comments: speed isn’t the same as quality or differentiation.If everyone uses the same tool, outputs start looking the same.The real edge is still taste, positioning, and refinement

What’s interesting is not how fast this gets done… but how it changes where the real work sits.If the output is instant, then the thinking behind it becomes the differentiator.
Speed is impressive, but taste and strategy will still separate great brands from average ones.

The “landlord is also your competitor” problem is real. I’ve seen companies grow fast… then hit a ceiling because their core dependency sits with a rival. This kind of deal isn’t about ownership. It’s about control over the one constraint that actually matters.

hook_text_english: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is live. For free. Indian marketers haven't noticed.
full_text_english: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is live. For free.And Indian marketers haven't noticed what just hit them. 🤯The generator era of AI images is over.Every old model had the same problem. Garbled text. Broken layouts. Hindi? Forget it.Here's what ChatGPT Images 2.0 can actually do:→ Renders Hindi and Bengali properly. For the first time ever inside an AI image.→ Browses the web mid-generation. Ask for an OpenAI merch poster, it pulls the actual products from the actual website.→ Builds slides, infographics, posters, UI screens. Not art. Design assets.→ Ratios from 3:1 wide to 1:3 tall. Built for LinkedIn banners and Instagram stories without cropping.→ 2K quality. Free tier included. Thinking mode for Plus, Pro, Business.The old models were brushes.This one is a designer that happens to use pixels.If you run a brand in India paying for 4 different design tools. Btw, time to rethink that stack.Thoughts?I share updates like these in my free AI community on WhatsApp.Join here ↓https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ

Hindi renders properly in an AI image and that’s somehow buried in the middle of the post. That deserved the headline. 😀Four design tools replaced by one prompt. Indian marketers aren’t sleeping on this — they just haven’t opened the tab yet.

Vaibhav Sisinty sir, I see you post, and I understand why the world is excited. It's a massive leap in generative AI.But here is a contrarian perspective. The real disruption for a business or a creator isn't just about generating "studio quality" images for free. The hardest problem this new capability introduces is actually how to protect your brand's identity and the integrity of your creative assets in a world where anyone can generate anything that looks like it was made by you.At Credenta, with our nine patents, we are solving a similar kind of problem for physical assets, ensuring structural integrity. But for your domain of digital growth, the next billion-dollar question is not about the quality of the generated output, but about establishing an immutable, verifiable chain of custody for AI-generated assets. How do you prove what was originally yours? How do you manage version control when every iteration is instant?I'd love to hear your take on this IP and authenticity challenge. In your experience, what is the biggest blind spot for creators when they start using these powerful tools at scale?

hook_text_english: AMD Developer showed blueprint for India's AI future at India AI Summit
full_text_english: hashtag#Sponsored_by_AMD India doesn't just want to use AI, it wants to own its AI future. And AMD Developer just showed up with the blueprint. At the India AI Summit, AMD brought something called Helios. An all-in-one AI supercomputer, but instead of filling up a building, it fits into server racks. Most powerful processors, latest AI chips, high-speed networking, full software layer, all working together out of the box. A single rack doing what only the world's top supercomputers could a few years ago. But here's the part that actually matters. It's completely open. No vendor lock-in. No proprietary walls. No one company controlling your stack. PyTorch, TensorFlow, your own tools - all plug in natively. Why does this matter for India? Because India is done renting AI from other countries' clouds. The goal is sovereign AI infrastructure, AI power that India owns, controls, and runs on its own soil. And this isn't a promise. It's proven. AMD already powers the world's most powerful supercomputers - Frontier, El Capitan in the U.S., and Arka and Arunika right here in India. Open infrastructure isn't a feature. It's the foundation of an entire country's AI ambition. If you want to learn more about AMD Developer AI solutions from Cloud to Datacenter to Consumer devices for your AI needs go to 3 And if you're someone who builds with AI or wants to start go check out the AMD AI Developer Program. It's free, it gives you access to AMD's tools and community, and it's built for builders at every level. Link in the pinned comment!

hook_text_english: The godfather of AI just said something I wish I hadn't read.
full_text_english: I don't know how to not think about this anymore.the godfather of AI just said something I wish I hadn't read.Geoffrey Hinton isn't saying AI will take your job. He's saying AI might take jobs as a concept.For 10,000 years, every tech revolution followed the same script. A machine killed one type of work, a new type appeared somewhere else.Tractors killed farm jobs. Factories opened. Factories closed. Offices opened. Offices got automated. Knowledge work opened.Every time one door closed, another one opened behind it. That's the only reason the economy kept working.Hinton's point is that AI isn't opening a new door. It's already standing behind every door you could possibly run to.Call center worker loses their job. Retrains as a coder. AI replaces the coder. Pivots to law. AI replaces law. Tries writing. AI replaces writing.There's no lane left to switch into.Goldman Sachs already estimates two-thirds of jobs in the US and Europe are exposed to AI. Entry-level hiring in AI-exposed fields is down 13% since ChatGPT launched. Job openings overall are down 30%.Humans spent 10,000 years breaking through limitations. Food scarcity. Distance. Physical labor. Every time we hit a ceiling, a bigger economy appeared above it.Hinton is saying human intelligence was the last ceiling. And maybe he's right.But here's what's also true. Every person reading this has access to tools today that didn't exist 3 years ago.The gap between someone who uses AI well and someone who doesn't is now wider than any degree ever was.That gap is the new door.Walk through it.vc: Milk Road AI
body_summary_english: Geoffrey Hinton warns AI could eliminate jobs as a concept, breaking historical cycle where automation creates new opportunities. Explores implications of AI-driven displacement without replacement job creation.

Understanding it might give a different perspective and help reduce unnecessary fear around AI and jobs.

Not even close.One post. One reshare from the right person. And suddenly you're in front of everyone who matters.No wonder Elon paid $44 billion to buy it.He wasn't buying a social network.He was buying the last room on the internet where real power still hangs out.

You pay the same bill every month.AI agents don't work like that.One engineer running Claude Code in an automated loop can drain a monthly budget in days.Multiply that across thousands of engineers. Every company fires workers to cut costs. Every fired worker stops buying products. Revenue collapses across every sector. The companies that fired everyone go bankrupt. It is a prisoner's dilemma. transcript: Sam Alman just dropped an AI that can literally use your computer for you. And Anthropic is quietly becoming the next Apple of AI. Same playbook. Once you are in, you never need to leave. This week alone, they launched three major updates, including a tool that might just replace Canva entirely. And this thing is so powerful, it could threaten the entire design industry and every office job built around making PPTs, websites, or apps. Meanwhile, Google was quietly cooking up the biggest week of all. They came for Hani Singh and every music producer in the country. They came for every coaching institute in India teaching J and Neat. And they built an AI that literally puts you inside any YouTube video you watch. Talk about main character energy. Mark Zuckerberg is quietly building an AI clone of himself to take meetings when he cannot show up. And Perplexity just launched an AI that works entirely while you sleep. And that is just five of the 14 AI updates from this week. Hi, I am Vibhav. I run three companies on AI and train people on AI in over 150 countries. Every week I cover the AI updates from around the world that are actually worth your time. Plus, at the end, I will show you how I used Clawude Design to turn a simple screenshot into a full working landing page, a motion graphics brand video, and a full pitch deck without ever opening PowerPoint. While I make this video once a week, if you want AI updates in real time, I have a WhatsApp community which is free to join and I drop AI updates in time there. Link is in the description. Let us go. Anthropic just launched Claude Design and this is not a mockup tool. Watch what it does. You describe what you want, attach your files and Claude builds the full design in front of you. In this demo, someone typed, "Design an interactive globe showing how culture flows between cities, attached a spreadsheet and a document, and Claude built a full dark themed 3D rotating globe with glowing paths and live statistics. Then they typed add controls and sliders appeared that change the colors in real time. It works for everything. App prototypes, presentation slides, landing pages, dashboards. You can tweak fonts and colors with sliders. Leave comments on specific parts of the design and toggle dark mode all through conversation. And when the design is done, you export it as a PDF, a PowerPoint. Send it straight to Canva or hand it off to Claude code to make it real. Available now on all paid Claude plans. So, Anthropic just dropped a Claude Opus 4.7, a proper upgrade over the Claude 4.6 you have been using. And two things are genuinely different. First, Opus 4.7 sees images in three times higher resolution than 4.6. So, when you upload a screenshot, a photo, or a messy document. It catches the small stuff the old version used to miss. On Anthropic's own testing, image accuracy jumped from 57 to 79%. Second, it is way better at handling multi-step tasks, the kind where you ask Claude to do one thing after another in sequence. So if you tell Claude to research a topic, turn the findings into a table and share it. 4.7 finishes the whole thing with one/ird the errors 4.6 used to make. And it is live right now across every paid Claude plan. No upgrade required. But honestly, the most interesting launch from Anthropic this week isn't the model itself. Claude just launched something called routines. You give it a task once and Claude runs it on its own servers on your schedule even while your laptop is closed. Basically, Claude automates work for you while you are away. We actually made a full 15minute tutorial breaking down exactly how routines work and how to set them up. So, if you want the complete guide, the video is linked here. OpenAI just dropped the biggest Codeex update of the year. And this is one update that isn't just for developers.
